title: Combat System — Class Design
description: Create a combat system using class inheritance. Define a Character base class with name, health, max_health, attack_power, and defense attributes, plus attack(target) and take_damage(amount) methods. Create an Enemy class that inherits from Character and adds a 'loot' attribute (an Item the enemy drops when defeated). Also create an is_alive() method on Character that returns True if health > 0.
test_code:
assert isinstance(Character, type), 'Character should be a class'
assert isinstance(Enemy, type), 'Enemy should be a class'
assert issubclass(Enemy, Character), 'Enemy should inherit from Character'
assert issubclass(Player, Character), 'Player should inherit from Character'
char = Character('Test', 50, 10, 3)
assert char.name == 'Test', f"Character.name wrong: {char.name}"
assert char.health == 50, f"Character.health wrong: {char.health}"
assert char.max_health == 50, f"Character.max_health wrong: {char.max_health}"
assert char.attack_power == 10, f"Character.attack_power wrong: {char.attack_power}"
assert char.defense == 3, f"Character.defense wrong: {char.defense}"
assert char.is_alive() == True, 'Character with health > 0 should be alive'
char.take_damage(50)
assert char.health == 0, f'Health should be 0 after taking 50 damage from 50, got {char.health}'
assert char.is_alive() == False, 'Character with 0 health should not be alive'
char2 = Character('A', 30, 12, 2)
target = Character('B', 40, 8, 5)
dmg = char2.attack(target)
assert dmg == 7, f'12 attack vs 5 defense should deal 7 damage, got {dmg}'
assert target.health == 33, f'Target health should be 33 after 7 damage from 40, got {target.health}'
weak = Character('W', 20, 3, 0)
strong_def = Character('S', 20, 0, 10)
weak_dmg = weak.attack(strong_def)
assert weak_dmg == 0, f'3 attack vs 10 defense should deal 0 damage, got {weak_dmg}'
test_loot = Item('gem', 'key', 'x')
enemy = Enemy('Rat', 10, 5, 1, loot=test_loot)
assert enemy.loot == test_loot, 'Enemy should have loot attribute'
assert enemy.get_loot() is None, 'Living enemy should not drop loot'
enemy.take_damage(10)
assert enemy.get_loot() == test_loot, 'Defeated enemy should drop loot'
enemy_no_loot = Enemy('Bat', 10, 3, 0)
assert enemy_no_loot.loot is None, 'Enemy without loot should have None'
over_dmg = Character('C', 10, 5, 0)
over_dmg.take_damage(999)
assert over_dmg.health == 0, f'Health should not go below 0, got {over_dmg.health}'
acceptance_criteria: Character class has name, health, max_health, attack_power, defense attributes
attack() deals max(0, attack_power - target.defense) and reduces target health
take_damage() reduces health but not below 0
Enemy inherits from Character and has a loot attribute
get_loot() returns loot only when enemy is defeated
hints: In take_damage: self.health = max(0, self.health - amount)
In attack: damage = max(0, self.attack_power - target.defense), then target.take_damage(damage)
Enemy.__init__ should call super().__init__(name, health, attack_power, defense)
Return the actual damage dealt from attack() — it's the value after the max(0, ...) calculation
